title: pulmonaria × norica
intro: Pulmonaria × norica, also known as Mountain Lungwort, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is a cross between Pulmonaria officinalis and Pulmonaria rubra. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Pulmonaria × norica is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and other health conditions.
